_meta.note: 12 event cards. One fires per world phase, weighted by scenario, drawn without replacement. Real instruments (REDATA, TFFF, PL 2780, ILO-169) are cited where true; the events themselves are fictional. Effects DSL documented in docs/model-notes.md.

events.name: Tariff wave
events.copy: Washington signals a new tariff schedule aimed at countries that condition inbound tech capital. Your conditioning agenda just acquired a price tag.

events.name: Export-control wave
events.copy: A fresh round of controls lands on advanced accelerators. Every coalition partner that depends on US compute rereads its contracts tonight.

events.name: Hyperscaler datacentre offer
events.copy: A hyperscaler proposes a multi-gigawatt campus on your grid. Brazil's REDATA regime exists for exactly this moment: terms attached now convert far better than terms begged for later.

events.name: Licensing conflict
events.copy: Your courts take up a training-data licensing case against a frontier lab, with PL 2780's copyright provisions in the background. The docket itself is leverage — for one news cycle.

events.name: Multilateral summit
events.copy: A working summit on AI and development actually works: communiqué with numbers in it, corridors full of ministers who return your calls. Recruiting is cheaper this turn.

events.name: BRICS technology bank
events.copy: Beijing circulates a draft charter for a BRICS technology bank — concessional compute, favourable terms, familiar institutions. The pull is real, and it is addressed to you.

events.name: Continental compute pact
events.copy: Washington floats a hemisphere-wide compute pact: preferential access for partners who align their data-centre rules. Your US-leaning partners find it hard to look away.

events.name: Minerals squeeze
events.copy: Processing bottlenecks bite and spot prices for niobium and heavy rare earths jump. Countries that converted their minerals position feel it as leverage; the rest feel it as weather.

events.name: Consultation halt
events.copy: A federal court pauses a datacentre over ILO-169 consultation requirements. The obligation is real; how it lands depends on whether your coalition built process before it built concrete.

events.name: Water conflict
events.copy: A cooling-water dispute puts a host municipality on the evening news, dry reservoirs behind the reporter. Public benefit stops being an abstraction.

events.name: Benefit audit
events.copy: An independent audit — TFFF-style, receipts and all — asks where the promised local benefit actually went. Coalitions that pooled with autocracies have the worst week.

events.name: Frontier leap
events.copy: A capability jump nobody scheduled. The poles' bypass options widen, and the gap between frontier and everyone else opens another notch.
